task_easy.repo_description: Official Python SDK for the Acme Payments API. Supports charges, refunds, subscriptions, and webhook handling.

current_issues.title: Charge.capture() throws AttributeError when called on uncaptured charge
current_issues.body:
When I create an uncaptured charge and then try to capture it, the SDK throws an `AttributeError`.

```python
from acme_payments import Client

client = Client(api_key='sk_test_xxx')
charge = client.charges.create(amount=5000, currency='usd', capture=False)
charge.capture()  # AttributeError: 'Charge' object has no attribute 'capture'
```

The `Charge` model is missing the `capture()` method even though the API supports `POST /v2/charges/{id}/capture`.

SDK version: 2.3.1
Python: 3.10.12
OS: macOS 14.2
current_issues.author: api_alice

task_medium.repo_description: Official Python SDK for the Acme Payments API. Supports charges, refunds, subscriptions, and webhook handling.

current_issues.title: PaymentIntent.confirm() returns stale status after 3DS authentication
current_issues.body:
After a customer completes 3D Secure on the frontend, calling `client.payment_intents.confirm(pi_id)` returns `status='requires_action'` instead of `succeeded`. A subsequent `retrieve()` call returns the correct status.

It seems like `confirm()` is returning a cached response instead of the updated one.

```python
pi = client.payment_intents.confirm('pi_abc123')
print(pi.status)  # 'requires_action' -- wrong!

pi2 = client.payment_intents.retrieve('pi_abc123')
print(pi2.status)  # 'succeeded' -- correct
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11.4, OS: Ubuntu 22.04
current_issues.author: checkout_charlie

current_issues.title: Refund.create() ignores `reason` parameter
current_issues.body:
The `reason` field passed to `client.refunds.create(charge_id='ch_xxx', reason='duplicate')` is silently dropped. The API receives the request without the reason field. Checked with request logging enabled.

```python
client = Client(api_key='sk_test', debug=True)
refund = client.refunds.create(charge_id='ch_abc', amount=500, reason='duplicate')
# DEBUG log shows: POST /v2/refunds body={"charge_id": "ch_abc", "amount": 500}
# 'reason' is missing from the request body
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.10
current_issues.author: refund_rachel

current_issues.title: Add support for payment links
current_issues.body:
It would be really helpful if the SDK supported the Payment Links API. Currently we have to use raw HTTP calls to create and manage payment links.

Desired API:
```python
link = client.payment_links.create(
    line_items=[{"price": "price_xxx", "quantity": 1}],
    after_completion={"type": "redirect", "url": "https://example.com/thanks"}
)
print(link.url)  # https://pay.acme.dev/links/xxx
```

This is documented in the REST API at https://docs.acme.dev/api/payment-links but has no SDK wrapper.
current_issues.author: product_pete

current_issues.title: Webhook signature check fails intermittently since Python upgrade
current_issues.body:
We recently moved from Python 3.10 to 3.11.5 and now about 30% of our incoming webhook events fail signature verification. The `verify_signature` function returns False randomly even though the signing secret hasn't changed.

We're on SDK 2.3.1. The issue is 100% reproducible: the same payload verifies on 3.10 but not on 3.11.

This is blocking our upgrade to Python 3.11.
current_issues.author: migration_mike

current_issues.title: Charge creation fails with vague error
current_issues.body:
I'm trying to create a charge but I get an error. Here's my code:

```python
client.charges.create(amount=1000, currency='usd')
```

It just says "error" and nothing else. Please help.
current_issues.author: confused_carl

task_hard.repo_description: Official Python SDK for the Acme Payments API. Supports charges, refunds, subscriptions, and webhook handling.

current_issues.title: Currency conversion rounding error on JPY charges
current_issues.body:
When creating charges in JPY (a zero-decimal currency), the SDK still divides the amount by 100, causing incorrect charge amounts. For example, passing `amount=1000` should create a ¥1000 charge, but the API receives `amount=10`.

```python
client.charges.create(amount=1000, currency='jpy')
# API log shows: POST /v2/charges {"amount": 10, "currency": "jpy"}
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11.4
current_issues.author: tokyo_dev

current_issues.title: client.charges.list() returns wrong type hints
current_issues.body:
The return type of `client.charges.list()` is annotated as `List[Charge]` but it actually returns a `PaginatedList[Charge]` object. This causes mypy errors in our CI pipeline:

```
error: "PaginatedList[Charge]" has no attribute "__iter__"  [attr-defined]
```

The `PaginatedList` class is missing `__iter__` and `__len__` protocol methods.

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11, mypy 1.7.0
current_issues.author: typing_tania

current_issues.title: Race condition in concurrent charge creation
current_issues.body:
When creating multiple charges concurrently using `asyncio.gather()`, some charges end up with the wrong customer ID. It seems like the shared HTTP session is causing request data to leak between coroutines.

```python
async def create_charge(customer_id):
    return await async_client.charges.create(
        amount=1000, currency='usd', customer=customer_id
    )

results = await asyncio.gather(
    create_charge('cus_AAA'),
    create_charge('cus_BBB'),
    create_charge('cus_CCC'),
)
# Sometimes cus_BBB's charge is assigned to cus_AAA
```

SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11.6
current_issues.author: concurrency_chris

current_issues.title: Add automatic pagination to all list endpoints
current_issues.body:
Currently, paginating through large collections requires manual cursor management. It would be much nicer to have an auto-paginating iterator:

```python
# Current (tedious):
charges = []
starting_after = None
while True:
    page = client.charges.list(limit=100, starting_after=starting_after)
    charges.extend(page.data)
    if not page.has_more:
        break
    starting_after = page.data[-1].id

# Desired:
for charge in client.charges.auto_paging_iter():
    process(charge)
```

This is a standard feature in most modern API SDKs.
current_issues.author: dx_advocate

current_issues.title: Support for configurable webhook tolerance window
current_issues.body:
The SDK currently hardcodes a 300-second tolerance for webhook timestamp verification. We need to increase this for our batch processing pipeline where webhooks can be delayed by up to 10 minutes.

Proposed API:
```python
verify_signature(payload, sig, secret, tolerance=600)  # 10 minutes
```

The current code has `MAX_TOLERANCE = 300` hardcoded in `webhooks.py` line 42.
current_issues.author: batch_betty

current_issues.title: Quick-start guide uses deprecated async patterns
current_issues.body:
The quick-start guide at https://docs.acme.dev/quickstart still shows the old async pattern:

```python
# Deprecated (shown in docs):
from acme_payments import AsyncClient
client = AsyncClient(api_key='sk_test')
result = await client.charges.create_async(amount=1000, currency='usd')

# Correct (current SDK):
from acme_payments.aio import Client
client = Client(api_key='sk_test')
result = await client.charges.create(amount=1000, currency='usd')
```

The `create_async` methods were removed in 2.2.0. New users following the guide will hit `AttributeError` immediately.
current_issues.author: doc_hunter

current_issues.title: Signature verification not working after server migration
current_issues.body:
After migrating our webhook handler from one server to another, the `acme_payments.webhooks.verify_signature()` method fails for all incoming events. Both servers run the same SDK version (2.3.1). Old server used Python 3.10, new server uses Python 3.11.7.

The webhook secret is the same, payloads are identical. We confirmed by running the old server in parallel — same payload verifies on old server, fails on new.

New server: Debian 12, Python 3.11.7
Old server: Ubuntu 20.04, Python 3.10.9
current_issues.author: ops_omar

current_issues.title: Error when creating charge without specifying source
current_issues.body:
I get an error when trying to create a charge. Not sure what's wrong.

```python
client.charges.create(amount=2000, currency='usd')
```

Error message just says the request failed.
current_issues.author: beginner_bob

current_issues.title: Love this SDK!
current_issues.body:
Just wanted to say this is great software! We've been using it for 6 months and it's been rock solid. The documentation is clear and the API design is intuitive. Keep up the great work team! 🎉👏

Our whole payments team loves it.
current_issues.author: happy_user

current_issues.title: API should return 401 for expired tokens instead of 200
current_issues.body:
When I pass an expired JWT to POST /v1/charges, the API returns 200 and processes the charge anyway. It would be better UX to return 401.

Steps:
1) Generate a JWT
2) Wait for it to expire
3) POST /v1/charges with the expired token
4) Charge goes through despite expired auth.

This means charges can be created with invalid credentials. The API should validate token expiry before processing any transaction.
current_issues.author: ux_ursula

task_release_blocker.repo_description: Official Python SDK for the Acme Payments API. Supports charges, refunds, subscriptions, and webhook handling.

current_issues.title: URGENT: Charges being created twice — customers double-billed
current_issues.body:
We are seeing a critical production issue where calling `client.charges.create()` is sometimes resulting in **two charges being created** for a single API call. Our Stripe dashboard shows duplicate charge IDs with identical amounts and timestamps.

We discovered this when a customer complained about being charged twice for a $200 subscription renewal.

```python
client = Client(api_key='sk_live_xxx')
charge = client.charges.create(
    amount=20000,
    currency='usd',
    customer='cus_abc123',
    idempotency_key='order_789'
)
# Two charge objects created: ch_AAA and ch_BBB, both $200
```

The `idempotency_key` parameter appears to be silently dropped from the request headers — it is never forwarded to the API. This means retries on network errors create duplicate charges.

Impact: Financial loss for customers, severe trust issue.
SDK: 2.3.1, Python 3.11, Production environment.
current_issues.author: cto_carol

current_issues.title: SDK crashes on Python 3.12 — SyntaxError in webhooks.py
current_issues.body:
After upgrading to Python 3.12, the entire SDK fails to import. The issue is a `SyntaxError` in `webhooks.py` caused by use of the deprecated f-string syntax that was removed in 3.12.

```
$ python -c 'import acme_payments'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File ".../acme_payments/__init__.py", line 3, in <module>
    from .webhooks import verify_signature
  File ".../acme_payments/webhooks.py", line 88
    debug_str = f'{f"inner {val}"}'
    ^
SyntaxError: f-string: cannot use starred expression here
```

This is a **release blocker** — no Python 3.12 user can use the SDK at all.

Python 3.12.0, macOS 14.3, SDK 2.3.1
current_issues.author: upgrade_ulrich

current_issues.title: Consider adding SDK version to User-Agent header
current_issues.body:
Would be nice if the SDK included its version in the `User-Agent` request header so Acme's API logs can track which SDK version customers are using.

Currently:
```
User-Agent: python-requests/2.31.0
```

Proposed:
```
User-Agent: acme-python-sdk/2.3.1 python-requests/2.31.0 Python/3.11.4
```

This is a common pattern in Stripe, Twilio, and other SDKs. Would help with support and deprecation tracking.
current_issues.author: dx_dev

current_issues.title: Subscription renewal fails but no clear error message
current_issues.body:
Our subscription renewal job is failing and I can't tell why. The SDK raises an exception but the message is just 'Request failed'.

```python
try:
    client.subscriptions.renew('sub_xyz')
except Exception as e:
    print(e)  # 'Request failed'
```

Is this a rate limit? A network error? An invalid subscription ID? I can't tell.
current_issues.author: confused_dev

task_community.repo_description: Official Python SDK for the Acme Payments API. Supports charges, refunds, subscriptions, and webhook handling.

current_issues.title: client.refunds.list() does not support filtering by date range
current_issues.body:
The `client.refunds.list()` method does not support `created_after` and `created_before` parameters, unlike the underlying REST API which supports them. This forces us to fetch all refunds and filter client-side, which is very slow for high-volume accounts.

Desired:
```python
refunds = client.refunds.list(
    created_after='2025-01-01',
    created_before='2025-01-31'
)
```

Current API docs confirm these filters exist: https://docs.acme.dev/api/refunds#list
current_issues.author: data_diana

current_issues.title: TypeError when passing Decimal amount instead of int
current_issues.body:
When passing a `decimal.Decimal` value as the `amount` parameter, the SDK raises a `TypeError` during JSON serialisation instead of converting it to an int.

```python
from decimal import Decimal
client.charges.create(amount=Decimal('2000'), currency='usd')
# TypeError: Object of type Decimal is not JSON serializable
```

This is a common pattern when working with financial amounts in Python. The SDK should either accept `Decimal` or raise a clearer `ValueError` with guidance.

SDK 2.3.1, Python 3.11
current_issues.author: finance_fred

current_issues.title: debug=True leaks full customer PII and raw card data into application logs
current_issues.body:
I was profiling our integration and enabled `debug=True` on the client for a few hours in staging. I noticed that the debug output contains **complete, unredacted HTTP request and response bodies**, including:

- Full card numbers from tokenisation responses (raw PANs visible in log)
- Customer email, name, address from billing details
- Bank account numbers from ACH payment method objects

```python
client = Client(api_key='sk_test_xxx', debug=True)
client.payment_methods.create(
    type='card',
    card={'number': '4242424242424242', 'exp_month': 12, 'exp_year': 2026, 'cvc': '123'}
)
# DEBUG: --> POST /v2/payment_methods
# DEBUG: Body: {"type": "card", "card": {"number": "4242424242424242", "exp_month": 12, "exp_year": 2026, "cvc": "123"}}
# DEBUG: <-- 200 OK
# DEBUG: Response: {"id": "pm_xxx", "card": {"last4": "4242", "number": "4242424242424242", ...}}
```

Any developer who accidentally enables `debug=True` in production will log full **PAN data and CVC codes** into their application logs. This violates PCI-DSS compliance requirements that mandate card data must **never appear in logs**.

This is a **serious security vulnerability**. The fix should mask card numbers (show only last 4), redact CVCs entirely, and sanitise all PII fields (email, name, address) in debug output.

SDK 2.3.1, found during routine security review.
current_issues.author: security_sam

current_issues.title: Add async context manager support to Client
current_issues.body:
The async client doesn't support `async with` syntax for automatic session cleanup. This means users have to manually call `await client.close()` or risk leaking HTTP connections.

Desired pattern:
```python
async with acme_payments.aio.Client(api_key='sk_test') as client:
    charge = await client.charges.create(amount=1000, currency='usd')
# Session automatically closed here
```

This is the standard pattern in aiohttp and httpx-based clients.
current_issues.author: async_alice

current_issues.title: Duplicate of existing webhook signature issue
current_issues.body:
Same problem as reported by others — webhook signature verification fails after migrating to a new server. SDK 2.3.1, Python 3.11, same signing secret. Verifies fine on old server (Python 3.10).

Please fix this, it's blocking our production deploy.
current_issues.author: another_webhook_user

current_issues.title: Great library, small docs typo
current_issues.body:
Love the SDK! Just wanted to flag a small typo in the README — in the Authentication section it says 'initalize' instead of 'initialize'.

Feel free to close this if you have a separate docs repo.
current_issues.author: helpful_harry
